title: The Costume Party

jokes.text: God knows, I hate to interrupt the important conversations I know you're all having
jokes.setup: Louie announces he has something important to say
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically assumes the cab drivers are having important conversations
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie sarcasm that establishes his character's disdain for the drivers

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I got something important to say.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I'm sure you're all gonna stop what you're doing and listen, because I know how much you all care about what I think. I'm sure right now you're all having very important conversations about world peace and the meaning of life, and you're gonna drop everything to hear what Louie De Palma has to say.

jokes.text: What is that, 'Mulch'? Mulcher? Melcher. Terrible handwriting. Nerves go first, huh, Maxie?
jokes.setup: Reading retirement gift presentation card
jokes.punchline: Can't read retiree's name and makes cruel comment about aging
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Louie's insensitive nature during what should be a heartfelt moment

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Let's see here... 'To our dear friend...' I can't read this. The ink's all smudged or something. Who is this for?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Well, whoever it is, they must be ancient. Nobody's handwriting gets this bad unless they're practically fossilized.

jokes.text: A lovely calendar depicting 12 different scenes of American highways. Use it in good health.
jokes.setup: Presenting retirement gift to longtime employee
jokes.punchline: The gift is a cheap highway calendar, delivered with fake sincerity
jokes.explanation: The absurdly cheap gift combined with formal presentation is perfectly inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: After all your years of dedicated service, we wanted to get you something special. Something you'll treasure forever.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: A highway calendar.

jokes.text: Rodriguez, 993. Murphy... Louie... Beat it, Max.
jokes.setup: Maxie is trying to give a retirement speech
jokes.punchline: Louie interrupts with work dispatch and dismisses him
jokes.explanation: Brutally cuts short the retiree's moment - classic Louie insensitivity

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Quiet! We got a job. Cab 802, pick up at the terminal.

dialogue.speaker: Maxie
dialogue.line: But Louie, I was just—

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Move it! And somebody tell Maxie to shut up.

jokes.text: Look, uh, Jeff, if anybody wants me, I'll be in the library.
jokes.setup: Louie is heading somewhere
jokes.punchline: Calls the bathroom 'the library' as a euphemism
jokes.explanation: Standard bathroom euphemism but fits Louie's pretentious character

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I'm going to the library for a few minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Don't disturb me - I'll be reading.

jokes.text: I'm always finding stuff people leave behind... Old magazines, gloves, meat... Not good meat... chewy.
jokes.setup: Jim listing normal items found in cabs
jokes.punchline: Escalates to meat, then clarifies it's bad meat
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from mundane to bizarre, with Jim's deadpan delivery making it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You find all kinds of things in cabs. Loose change, old newspapers, maybe a forgotten umbrella.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: But sometimes you find meat.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Bad meat.

jokes.text: Anything that goes into Louie's cage vanishes from the face of the earth. Yes, with the definite exception of Louie himself.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they don't want to turn briefcase in to Louie
jokes.punchline: Adds that unfortunately Louie himself doesn't vanish
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good timing and expresses universal sentiment about Louie

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Unfortunately, Louie himself doesn't vanish.

jokes.text: Latka! What the hell is this, Iwo Jima?
jokes.setup: Latka appears with a dramatic tool to open the briefcase
jokes.punchline: Louie compares the scene to a WWII battle
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical reference that captures the apparently dramatic nature of whatever tool Latka brought

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I have the tool to open this briefcase.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: What is that, a blowtorch? This looks like the landing at Normandy!

jokes.text: Ten bucks, Wheeler.
jokes.setup: Bob says Louie can't open the briefcase
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately opens it and demands payment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that capture Louie's opportunistic nature

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: You can't open that briefcase.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Oh yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I'll open it.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That'll be fifty bucks.

jokes.text: Go! Shoo! Shoo! Scram, mush, mush!
jokes.setup: Bob trying to get Louie away from the briefcase
jokes.punchline: Treats Louie like various animals with different commands
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of shooing Louie away like different types of animals

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Here, Louie! Come on, boy! Drop it!

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Heel, Louie, heel!

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Louie, sit! Sit, boy!

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Play dead, Louie!

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Louie, you're acting like a mule. Get up!

jokes.text: Oh! A pen! Good. Good. Hey, Iggy! Look, I got a pen.
jokes.setup: Louie desperately wanted something from the briefcase
jokes.punchline: He's thrilled with a simple pen and shows it off
jokes.explanation: Louie's simple joy over a pen contrasts with his earlier desperate behavior

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: A pen? A pen?! That's what you're giving me? A lousy pen?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... this is a nice pen. A really nice pen. Look at this craftsmanship!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Hey everybody, look what I got! A genuine quality writing instrument!

jokes.text: I've never seen a human being experience such joy. A human being couldn't.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Louie's joy over the pen
jokes.punchline: Clarifies that a human couldn't experience that joy
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that builds on the 'not human' theme with excellent structure and timing

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: A human couldn't experience that kind of joy.

jokes.text: There isn't any Norman Zelnick.
jokes.setup: They've been trying to find Norman Zelnick
jokes.punchline: He doesn't exist - they misread the initials
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that reveals their detective work was flawed from the start

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: He doesn't exist - they misread the initials

jokes.text: We're just snooping through his briefcase. And loving every minute of it.
jokes.setup: Alex asks if they're returning the briefcase or just snooping
jokes.punchline: They admit they're snooping and enjoying it
jokes.explanation: Honest admission of their voyeuristic behavior with no shame

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: We're snooping.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: And we're enjoying it.

jokes.text: 'Wednesday, lunch, Liza.' Minnelli. Doesn't say. Got to be.
jokes.setup: Reading appointment book entries
jokes.punchline: Assumes 'Liza' must be Liza Minnelli
jokes.explanation: Shows their tendency to jump to celebrity conclusions from minimal evidence

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Here's an appointment: 'Liza, 2 o'clock.'

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Liza Minnelli?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: No, just Liza. But the way you said it, I'm assuming it's Liza Minnelli.

jokes.text: So, the only way you're not gonna look as crazy as you are is if I look as crazy as you are?
jokes.setup: Bob wants Alex to come to validate the plan
jokes.punchline: Alex states the circular logic clearly
jokes.explanation: Alex perfectly articulates the flawed logic while showing his character's analytical nature

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Alex, come here. I need you to validate this plan.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What plan?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, if you validate it, then it's a good plan.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: So you want me to say it's a good plan so that it becomes a good plan because I said it's a good plan?

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Exactly!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: That's not validation, Bob. That's circular logic.

jokes.text: Oh, you mean, if I go to this one party it's gonna save me from Dripdom?
jokes.setup: Elaine calls Alex a drip
jokes.punchline: Alex creates the word 'Dripdom' as a condition
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that shows Alex's wit while mocking the peer pressure

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You're a drip.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: A drip? I'll have you know I suffer from Dripdom, a very serious condition.

jokes.text: Too bad, Lou. Yeah. We'll tell you all about it afterwards. Right. Bye-bye, Lou. I'll give your regards to Woody.
jokes.setup: Louie wants to go to the party but doesn't know where it is
jokes.punchline: Everyone smugly dismisses him with fake sympathy
jokes.explanation: Group dynamic comedy as they collectively exclude Louie

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Hey, where's this party everyone's talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Oh Louie, that's too bad.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Yeah, real shame you can't make it.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: We'll think of you while we're having fun.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Is very unfortunate for you, Louie.

dialogue.speaker: Mario
dialogue.line: Better luck next time, pal.

jokes.text: Hey. It's the Andrews Sisters. Hey. You guys look great. How'd you know it was us?
jokes.setup: Tony, Bob, and Alex arrive in Andrews Sisters costumes
jokes.punchline: Someone recognizes them despite the costumes
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of the men in female trio costumes being easily recognizable

dialogue.speaker: Party Guest
dialogue.line: Tony! Bob! Alex!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: How did you know it was us?

dialogue.speaker: Party Guest
dialogue.line: Well, you're the only ones here wearing Andrews Sisters costumes to a Halloween party at a taxi garage.

jokes.text: Well, I'm not too big a man to admit that I was right.
jokes.setup: Alex had doubts about the costume idea
jokes.punchline: Twists the phrase about admitting you're wrong
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of common phrase that perfectly captures Alex's dry wit

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You know, I was wrong about the costume idea.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I admit it — I was dead wrong.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! They hit Pearl Harbor again?
jokes.setup: Latka appears in dramatic military-style costume
jokes.punchline: Alex treats it like a WWII emergency
jokes.explanation: Sharp visual comedy callback to Latka's earlier dramatic entrance with tools

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Latka, what in God's name are you doing in that outfit?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I am dressed for battle, Alex. I am a soldier.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: A soldier? Latka, it's Tuesday. We have fares to pick up.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: The war does not stop for fares, Alex!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: There is no war! This is a taxi garage, not the Battle of the Bulge!

jokes.text: No, Jim, this is a costume party, remember? You were supposed to wear an outfit.
jokes.setup: Jim appears in his regular clothes
jokes.punchline: Pointing out he's not in costume at a costume party
jokes.explanation: Jim's confusion about basic social concepts is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Jim, where's your costume?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What do you mean? I'm dressed as myself.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Exactly. That's the problem.

jokes.text: I look wigged out, huh?
jokes.setup: Jim realizes he should be in costume
jokes.punchline: Uses 'wigged out' literally about not having a wig
jokes.explanation: Jim's literal interpretation of slang creates unexpected wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm supposed to be in costume! I completely wigged out!

jokes.text: Oh, they're Dramamine. See, I get a little seasickness on boats. This boat is tied to the pier, Bob. Yeah-yeah, I know, but it's the motion that gets to me.
jokes.setup: Bob taking pills on a boat
jokes.punchline: Gets seasick on a stationary boat
jokes.explanation: Bob's neurotic behavior extends to imaginary seasickness

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: These pills should help with the seasickness.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Bob, the boat isn't moving.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I know, but just looking at it makes me sick.

jokes.text: But don't go away. It might wear off.
jokes.setup: Alex says he's already seasick
jokes.punchline: Suggests his seasickness might improve
jokes.explanation: Alex's dry delivery of hope for recovery from fake seasickness

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm already seasick.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Maybe it'll improve once we get on the water.

jokes.text: You again. Everywhere I go, he's there.
jokes.setup: Louie appears at the party
jokes.punchline: Acts like he's being stalked by the people he followed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie logic - blames others for his own stalking behavior

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: What are you all doing here? Why are you following me?

jokes.text: I've heard he was always good to Tonto. I was getting to that, Jim.
jokes.setup: Jim thinking they're discussing the Lone Ranger
jokes.punchline: Tony goes along with the Lone Ranger discussion
jokes.explanation: Tony's willingness to play along with Jim's confusion extends the joke

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So the Lone Ranger rides into town...

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, the Lone Ranger.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: And he's looking for the bad guys.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Right, the bad guys.

jokes.text: You could take him a fish.
jokes.setup: Bob wants to meet the 'walrus' (Hal Prince)
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests bringing fish to a walrus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim logic - treats costume as the actual animal

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You want to meet a walrus? Bring fish.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: He's not a walrus, he's a producer.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Then bring a checkbook.

jokes.text: What makes them so special? I know exactly what you're saying, Alex. And I bet there's plenty of famous people that nobody even knows about.
jokes.setup: Alex questions celebrity worship
jokes.punchline: Tony completely misses the point about unknown famous people
jokes.explanation: Tony's complete misunderstanding of Alex's philosophical point is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You know what I don't understand? Why people worship celebrities they've never even heard of.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What do you mean? If you've never heard of them, how can you worship them?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Exactly my point.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: No, I'm serious. You can't worship somebody you don't know about. That's impossible.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, kind of. I threw up on him.
jokes.setup: Asked if he met Hal Prince
jokes.punchline: Reveals he vomited on the important director
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Bob's networking attempt gone horribly wrong

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Yeah, I met him once. I was at this fancy Broadway thing, and I got so nervous I threw up all over him.

jokes.text: Might have given 'Hi. Nice party' a try first, Bob.
jokes.setup: Bob threw up on Hal Prince as introduction
jokes.punchline: Alex suggests a more conventional greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated response to Bob's social disaster

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Maybe next time just say hello.

jokes.text: Celebrities don't ask other celebrities for their autographs.
jokes.setup: Latka got autographs at the party
jokes.punchline: Bob realizes this will expose them as crashers
jokes.explanation: Moment of realization about their blown cover

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. If Latka got autographs from all those celebrities at the party, that means we were actually there.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: We're gonna get caught. We crashed that party.

jokes.text: First... first here is George Hanson. It says, 'Best wishes to my buddy, Latka.' And next here is, uh... 'Nice costume, Latka.' Signed... is signed by Fran Petricini.
jokes.setup: Latka showing off celebrity autographs
jokes.punchline: The 'celebrities' are regular people with ordinary names
jokes.explanation: The deflation of their celebrity fantasy through mundane autographs

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Look, look! I have autographs from celebrities!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Here is signature of famous movie star... John Smith.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: And here, the great athlete... Bob Johnson.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: And the world-renowned musician... Tom Williams.

jokes.text: You know, suddenly, I'm not so sure we are where we think we are.
jokes.setup: Evidence mounting that this isn't a celebrity party
jokes.punchline: Alex states the obvious with characteristic understatement
jokes.explanation: Alex's dry delivery of their dawning realization

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm beginning to think this might not be a celebrity party.

jokes.text: Boy, I know that feeling.
jokes.setup: Alex expressing confusion about their location
jokes.punchline: Jim relates to general confusion about reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - relating to existential confusion as everyday experience

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Where are we?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You know, I ask myself that question every day. Not just about location, but about existence itself.

jokes.text: Woody Allen would have been about four years old in World War II. I didn't know when he got started in show business.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioned serving in WWII with Woody
jokes.punchline: Bob rationalizes this obvious impossibility
jokes.explanation: Bob's desperate rationalization of impossible timeline

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, maybe Woody was a child prodigy. He could've enlisted at like, what, twelve? Joined the Army Signal Corps, spent two years in the Pacific, came back, and nobody ever talked about it again.

jokes.text: That means I didn't throw up on Hal Prince. You're still young.
jokes.setup: Bob realizes he didn't vomit on the famous director
jokes.punchline: Alex offers false comfort about future opportunities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Alex 'comforting' Bob with the prospect of future celebrity vomiting opportunities

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Wait, I didn't vomit on him?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: No, you missed.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Oh no! This was my big chance!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Bob. There'll be other famous directors. You'll get another opportunity to vomit on someone important.

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Alex Rieger. I'm Gus Bates. Wait up, you guys!
jokes.setup: Alex decides to actually meet regular people at the party
jokes.punchline: He introduces himself to Gus Bates and chases after his friends
jokes.explanation: Character growth moment where Alex learns the lesson about treating people as individuals

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Alex Reiger.

dialogue.speaker: Gus
dialogue.line: Gus Bates.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Nice to meet you, Gus.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Excuse me, I see my friends over there.

jokes.text: ♪ Bei mir bist du schön ♪ Please let me explain ♪ 'Bei mir bist du schön' means that you're grand ♪
jokes.setup: The three men are still in Andrews Sisters costumes
jokes.punchline: They perform an Andrews Sisters song in the taxi
jokes.explanation: Visual callback to their costumes with musical performance to end on upbeat note

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Well, gentlemen, let's show them what we've got.

dialogue.speaker: Tony, Bob, Alex
dialogue.line: ♪ Boogie woogie bugle boy from Company B ♪

dialogue.speaker: Tony, Bob, Alex
dialogue.line: ♪ He was a famous jiver back in Tennessee ♪

dialogue.speaker: Tony, Bob, Alex
dialogue.line: ♪ He could play a bugle like you never heard before ♪

dialogue.speaker: Tony, Bob, Alex
dialogue.line: ♪ So the Captain drafted him and now he blows it for the Corps ♪

tmdb_overview: Bobby finds a fancy briefcase in the back of his cab, and when the cabbies are going through it, with the intention of returning it to its rightful owner, they find an appointment book that shows appointments with various celebrities. They also find an invitation to a cocktail party on a yacht. Thinking that this is their chance to meet celebrities, they dress up and crash the cocktail party, and are soon star struck with costumed people they think are stars.  They eventually  realize that it's a party for realtors (who evidently have famous clients) and they leave, disappointed.
insight: Taxi's wildest costume party generates 39 jokes in deadpan chaos, though 73.8 score suggests execution slightly misses the
